source_pr.title: tools/bin/ut: support generate JUnit format test report
source_pr.body:
<!--

Thank you for contributing to TiDB!

PR Title Format:
1. pkg [, pkg2, pkg3]: what's changed
2. *: what's changed

-->

### What problem does this PR solve?
<!--

Please create an issue first to describe the problem.

There MUST be one line starting with "Issue Number:  " and 
linking the relevant issues via the "close" or "ref".

For more info, check https://pingcap.github.io/tidb-dev-guide/contribute-to-tidb/contribute-code.html#referring-to-an-issue.

-->

Issue Number: close #30822

Problem Summary:

In the old CI script, we support generate the JUnit format test report

https://github.com/pingcap/tidb/blob/453535600f6016b4faae3158277f92013f111a85/Makefile#L142-L143

Before we can replace the old script with the new one, `make ut` should support this feature.

### What is changed and how it works?

Support `--junitfile xxx` flags for `make ut`, the test report will be generated in the JUnit file format, compatibility with the old script.

### Check List

Tests <!-- At least one of them must be included. -->

- [ ] Unit test
- [ ] Integration test
- [x] Manual test (add detailed scripts or steps below)


```
make ut X='run tablecodec --junitfile xxx'
cat xxx
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<testsuites>
        <testsuite tests="19" failures="0" time="0.000000" name="github.com/pingcap/tidb/tablecodec">
                <properties>
                        <property name="go.version" value="go1.16.8 linux/amd64"></property>
                </properties>
                <testcase classname="github.com/pingcap/tidb/tablecodec" name="TestCutPrefix" time=""></testcase>
                <testcase classname="github.com/pingcap/tidb/tablecodec" name="TestPrefix" time=""></testcase>
                <testcase classname="github.com/pingcap/tidb/tablecodec" name="TestRecordKey" time=""></testcase>
                <testcase classname="github.com/pingcap/tidb/tablecodec" name="TestRange" time=""></testcase>
                <testcase classname="github.com/pingcap/tidb/tablecodec" name="TestUntouchedIndexKValue" time=""></testcase>
                <testcase classname="github.com/pingcap/tidb/tablecodec" name="TestDecodeAutoIDMeta" time=""></testcase>
                <testcase classname="github.com/pingcap/tidb/tablecodec" name="TestError" time=""></testcase>
                <testcase classname="github.com/pingcap/tidb/tablecodec" name="TestRowCodec" time=""></testcase>
                <testcase classname="github.com/pingcap/tidb/tablecodec" name="TestTimeCodec" time=""></testcase>
                <testcase classname="github.com/pingcap/tidb/tablecodec" name="TestDecodeBadDecical" time=""></testcase>
                <testcase classname="github.com/pingcap/tidb/tablecodec" name="TestTableCodecInvalid" time=""></testcase>
                <testcase classname="github.com/pingcap/tidb/tablecodec" name="TestCutRow" time=""></testcase>
                <testcase classname="github.com/pingcap/tidb/tablecodec" name="TestDecodeIndexKey" time=""></testcase>
                <testcase classname="github.com/pingcap/tidb/tablecodec" name="TestCutKey" time=""></testcase>
                <testcase classname="github.com/pingcap/tidb/tablecodec" name="TestUnflattenDatums" time=""></testcase>
                <testcase classname="github.com/pingcap/tidb/tablecodec" name="TestTableCodec" time=""></testcase>
                <testcase classname="github.com/pingcap/tidb/tablecodec" name="TestIndexKey" time=""></testcase>
                <testcase classname="github.com/pingcap/tidb/tablecodec" name="TestCutKeyNew" time=""></testcase>
                <testcase classname="github.com/pingcap/tidb/tablecodec" name="TestDecodeColumnValue" time=""></testcase>
        </testsuite>
</testsuites>
```


- [ ] No code

Side effects

- [ ] Performance regression: Consumes more CPU
- [ ] Performance regression: Consumes more Memory
- [ ] Breaking backward compatibility

Documentation

- [ ] Affects user behaviors
- [ ] Contains syntax changes
- [ ] Contains variable changes
- [ ] Contains experimental features
- [ ] Changes MySQL compatibility

### Release note

<!-- bugfix or new feature needs a release note -->

```release-note
None
```

symptoms: "select create_time from mysql.stats_history where table_id = '%d' order by create_time desc limit 1) "+ (executor/analyze_test.go)
failure_signature: "select create_time from mysql.stats_history where table_id = '%d' order by create_time desc limit 1) "+ (executor/analyze_test.go)
root_cause_explanation: The test was flaky because it relied on nondeterministic result ordering (e.g., missing ORDER BY / unsorted assertions). Result ordering is not guaranteed unless explicitly enforced. This PR stabilizes it by: Sort results / enforce deterministic ordering before assertions to avoid nondeterministic flakiness.
fix_pattern: Sort results / enforce deterministic ordering before assertions to avoid nondeterministic flakiness.

evidence: evidence: "select create_time from mysql.stats_history where table_id = '%d' order by create_time desc limit 1) "+ (executor/analyze_test.go)

files: executor/analyze.go, executor/analyze_test.go, executor/infoschema_reader_test.go, session/bootstrap.go, session/bootstrap_test.go, statistics/handle/dump.go, statistics/handle/dump_test.go, statistics/handle/handle.go, statistics/handle/handle_test.go, tools/check/ut.go
